title: 10 best herbal creams for jock itch

remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil (Melaleuca alternifolia): Has potent antifungal and antibacterial properties that help combat fungal infections such as jock itch by directly targeting the causative organisms.

Aloe Vera: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which soothe itching, redness, and irritation caused by jock itch, while also promoting wound healing and skin regeneration.

Calendula (Calendula officinalis): Exhibits antifungal and antibacterial properties that help reduce the severity of jock itch symptoms, including inflammation, itching, and crusting, while also promoting tissue repair and collagen production.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with jock itch because of its potent antifungal properties. The active compound, cineole, in tea tree oil has been shown to effectively combat fungal infections, including those that cause jock itch. Tea tree cream creates an environment that is not conducive to fungal growth, thereby promoting healing and reducing the severity of symptoms such as itching, redness, and irritation. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective solution for alleviating jock itch symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This compound helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, making it less susceptible to fungal infections that cause jock itch.
Cymene: This terpene has antimicrobial properties, which help to eliminate fungal spores and bacteria that cause jock itch, promoting a healthy skin environment.
Linalool: This terpene has antifungal and antiseptic properties, helping to combat the fungus Trichophyton, a common cause of jock itch, and reduce the risk of infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Melaleuca alternifolia leaves are used due to their high content of tea tree oil, which has antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections like jock itch.
Buds: Melaleuca alternifolia buds are used for their high concentration of tea tree oil, which provides antiseptic and antifungal properties to soothe and heal jock itch affected areas.
Stems: Melaleuca alternifolia stems are used for their tea tree oil, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and itching associated with jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of unrefined shea butter and 100 grams of beeswax into a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Melt the shea butter and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for about 10 minutes.
Add 25 grams of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to the melted mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature.
Once solid, whip the cream with a hand mixer for about 5 minutes until it becomes light and fluffy.

remedy_properties: Soothing and anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's soothing properties help reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by jock itch, making it easier to manage symptoms.

Antifungal and antibacterial: Herbal aloe creams contain antifungal and antibacterial compounds that help combat fungal infections (like Candida) and bacterial overgrowth associated with jock itch, promoting healing and preventing further infection.

Moisturizing and barrier-forming: Aloe vera's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture and create a protective barrier on the skin, reducing dryness and irritation that can exacerbate jock itch symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with jock itch because of its soothing and antifungal properties. The gel extracted from the plant has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and itching caused by the fungal infection. Aloe vera also has antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of fungi, promoting a healthy environment for skin recovery. Regular application of aloe vera creams can help alleviate symptoms of jock itch, such as itching, burning, and cracking, and can aid in faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone compound has anti-fungal properties that inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for jock itch, such as Trichophyton rubrum.
Bradykinin: A peptide found in Aloe vera, bradykinin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with jock itch.
Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds, particularly epicatechin and epigallocatechin, have antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects that help combat jock itch infections and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of aloe vera gel, which provides soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.

Gel extracted from Leaves: More specifically, the clear jelly extracted from aloe vera leaves, often referred to as aloe vera gel, is commonly used to treat jock itch due to its antifungal and antibacterial properties.

Aloe vera sap (extracted from the Leaves): The sap, also known as aloe vera juice, is used in creams for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and heal the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 aloe vera leaves from mature plants and cut them from the base to ensure a steady supply.
Extract the gel from the aloe vera leaves using a spoon to scoop out the clear inner pulp.
Mix the aloe vera gel with 1/2 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of vegetable glycerin in a bowl.
Add 2 teaspoons of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the mixture and heat it gently.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and forms a smooth, creamy texture using an electric mixer.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower contains compounds like caryophyllene and terpinen-4-ol, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch.

Antifungal properties: The cream's antifungal compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, help combat the fungal infection Candida albicans, which is a common cause of jock itch, by inhibiting its growth and reproduction.

Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower's antioxidants, including polyphenols and flavonoids, help protect skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of jock itch.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with jock itch because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and polyphenols, help to combat fungal infections like jock itch, caused by Trichophyton and other pathogens. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy immune response, Echinacea purpurea creams can help to alleviate symptoms like itching, redness, and discomfort associated with jock itch, providing relief and promoting faster healing.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and fight fungal infections, which are key factors in the development and persistence of jock itch.
Flavonoids (specifically, Kaempferol and Quercetin): As potent antioxidants, flavonoids help to inhibit the growth of fungi and reduce oxidative stress, thereby alleviating symptoms of jock itch.

Triterpene Glycosides (specifically, Alkylamides and Cichoric Acid): These compounds exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to boost the body's immune response and reduce the severity of jock itch symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are used because they contain a high concentration of echinacosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe skin irritations.
Leaves: Leaves are used because they contain compounds like caffeic acid and chicoric acid, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal infections.
Roots (again, as it's the most used part): Roots are also used because they contain a high concentration of polysaccharides, which stimulate the immune system and help speed up the healing process.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours or air-dry for 7-10 days.
Steep 1/2 cup of dried echinacea purpurea in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and mix it with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to create a base.
Heat the base mixture in a double boiler until it reaches 180°f and then pour it into glass jars to cool and solidify.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch, making it easier to manage symptoms.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in pot marigold cream work to combat fungal infections, such as Malassezia furfur, which causes jock itch, by inhibiting their growth and preventing further infection.
Emollient properties: Pot marigold cream's emollient nature helps soothe and moisturize the affected skin, reducing irritation and discomfort while also promoting healthy skin regeneration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with jock itch because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help soothe and calm the affected area. The cream's ability to reduce redness and swelling makes it an effective treatment for jock itch caused by fungal infections such as ringworm and athlete's foot. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help prevent the spread of the infection, promoting faster healing and relief from discomfort. This natural remedy provides a safe and gentle solution for those suffering from jock itch.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help with jock itch by exhibiting antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce fungal growth and soothe irritated skin.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Calendula officinalis creams have antifungal and antioxidant properties, which can help combat fungal infections and promote wound healing in the affected area.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Calendula officinalis creams have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce inflammation, soothe irritated skin, and promote wound healing, ultimately helping to alleviate jock itch symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of triterpenoids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and heal skin irritations.
Leaves: They contain similar compounds to the flowers and are used for their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help combat fungal infections like jock itch.
Stems: They also contain triterpenoids and flavonoids, making them a useful addition to creams for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried calendula flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl to remove the solids.
Combine 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add the infused liquid to the melted wax and oil mixture stirring constantly for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Sesame oil contains antifungal compounds like sesamin and sesamolin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens like Malassezia furfur, the fungus responsible for jock itch.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and polyphenols present in sesame oil have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch.

Wound-healing properties: Sesame oil's emollient and moisturizing properties can promote wound healing by creating a protective barrier on the skin, reducing friction and irritation that can exacerbate jock itch symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesamum indicum, also known as sesame, creams helps with jock itch because of its antifungal properties, which effectively target and eliminate fungal infections that cause the condition. The cream's active ingredients, including sesamin and sesamolin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, reducing redness, itching, and discomfort associated with jock itch. Regular application of Sesamum indicum creams can provide long-lasting relief and promote the healing of affected skin, making it an effective treatment option for those suffering from this common condition.
remedy_constituents: Sesamin: It helps with jock itch by exhibiting antifungal properties, inhibiting the growth of fungi such as Trichophyton mentagrophytes, which causes the infection.
Sesamolin: It contributes to the antifungal activity of Sesamum indicum creams by displaying strong inhibitory effects on various fungal species, including Candida albicans.
Terpene (sesaminol triglyceride): It has been shown to possess antifungal activity, preventing the spread of jock itch by reducing the growth and proliferation of pathogenic fungi.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Sesame seeds are often used in creams for jock itch due to their anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties.
Leaves: Sesame leaves are used for their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help in soothing and treating jock itch.
Roots: Sesame roots are used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which aid in the treatment of jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried sesamum indicum flowers and store them in an airtight container for later use.
Mix 200 grams of sesamum indicum flowers with 400 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional stirring every 3 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh to separate the oil from the solids.
Add 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of vitamin e oil to the oil mixture and melt the beeswax in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch.

Antifungal properties: The flavonoids and terpenes in chamomile have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms that cause jock itch, such as Malassezia furfur and Trichophyton mentagrophytes.

Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's flavonoids and essential oils help calm irritated skin, reduce discomfort, and promote a sense of relaxation and reduced stress – all of which can exacerbate jock itch symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with jock itch because it has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The soothing and calming effects of chamomile can help reduce redness, itching, and irritation associated with jock itch. The antifungal properties in chamomile creams can also target and eliminate fungal infections, such as those caused by Trichophyton and Epidermophyton, that often lead to jock itch. This makes chamomile creams a natural and effective treatment option for individuals suffering from jock itch.
remedy_constituents: Apolipophorin E (ApE): This flavonoid glycoside has antifungal properties, which can help inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause jock itch.
Apigenin: As a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antifungal activities, apigenin may help reduce the severity of jock itch symptoms by alleviating inflammation and preventing fungal overgrowth.

Matricaria chamomilla Flavonoids (including Isoquercetin and Quercetin): These flavonoids possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the affected skin, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress caused by fungal infections.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make creams for jock itch due to their anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties.
Seeds: They are used to make creams for jock itch due to their antifungal and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for jock itch due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried chamomilla flowers, 100 grams of distilled water, and 50 grams of beeswax for preparation.
Infuse the dried chamomilla flowers in the distilled water for 10 minutes at 100 degrees celsius.
Strain the infused mixture and discard the solids then add 100 grams of sweet almond oil for emulsification.
Mix the sweet almond oil and infused mixture with 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler at 70 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken then transfer it to a clean container for storage.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Wintergreen cream's salicylic acid content helps reduce inflammation and itching associated with jock itch, providing relief to affected skin areas.
Antifungal properties: The menthol in wintergreen cream disrupts fungal growth, inhibiting the spread of Candida albicans and other fungi that cause jock itch.
Antibacterial properties: Wintergreen's eucalyptol content has antibacterial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate or complicate jock itch.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens, also known as wintergreen, creams helps with jock itch because of its anti-fungal properties. The herb contains methyl salicylate, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause jock itch. The cream also helps to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing inflammation and itching. Additionally, Gaultheria procumbens has astringent properties that help to dry out the affected area, preventing the fungus from spreading and promoting healing. This makes it a popular natural remedy for jock itch.
remedy_constituents: Methyl salicylate (MeSA): It helps reduce inflammation and relieve itching associated with jock itch by acting as a salicylate, a compound that inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.
Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds help reduce inflammation, kill fungal pathogens, and soothe irritated skin, making them beneficial for treating jock itch.
Gaultherin: Although less studied than other constituents, Gaultherin, a sesquiterpene lactone, has shown antifungal activity, which can help combat fungal infections causing jock itch.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams for jock itch as they contain compounds with antifungal properties, which can help combat fungal infections.
Roots: Used to make creams for jock itch as they contain salicin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and reduce irritation.
Barks: Used to make creams for jock itch as they contain compounds with antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the growth of pathogens and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and flowers on a sunny day with a clean knife.
Steep the harvested gaultheria procumbens in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create an infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids to obtain a liquid extract.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler with gentle heat.
Blend 2 tablespoons of the gaultheria procumbens infusion into the melted beeswax mixture with a hand mixer until smooth.

remedy_properties: Astringent Properties: Witch Hazel's astringent properties help to dry out and reduce itching and inflammation caused by Jock Itch, making it an effective antifungal agent.

Antimicrobial Properties: The antimicrobial compounds in Witch Hazel cream, such as tannins and polyphenols, inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause Jock Itch, preventing further infection and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: Witch Hazel's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with Jock Itch, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana, also known as American witch hazel, creams helps with jock itch because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The cream soothes and calms irritated skin, reducing itching and discomfort. The witch hazel's astringent properties help to reduce redness and inflammation, while its antifungal properties combat the fungal infections that cause jock itch. This natural remedy provides relief from the symptoms of jock itch, promoting healthy and balanced skin. Regular application can help to clear up the infection and prevent future occurrences.
remedy_constituents: Hamamelitannin: This tannin, a type of phenolic compound, has antimicrobial and astringent properties, which can help reduce fungal growth and soothe the affected skin area.

Vasoprotectives (e.g., amentoflavone, hamamelone): These compounds have vasoconstrictive and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce blood flow to the affected area and alleviate itching and redness associated with jock itch.

Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid, sinapic acid): These compounds have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which can help combat fungal infections and reduce oxidative stress in the skin, promoting healing and soothing the affected area.
remedy_parts: Barks: Hamamelis virginiana barks are often used to make creams for jock itch due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Hamamelis virginiana are also used in creams for jock itch due to their astringent and antiseptic properties that help to reduce itching and inflammation.
Roots: The roots of Hamamelis virginiana are used to make creams for jock itch due to their ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itching and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried hamamelis virginiana bark and 1 liter of distilled water to create an herbal infusion.
Steep the dried hamamelis virginiana bark in the distilled water for 2 hours at 100 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal liquid.
Combine 200 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil with the herbal liquid in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes then remove and let cool before whipping into a creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: The antimicrobial properties of onion cream help to combat fungal infections such as candida and yeast, which are common causes of jock itch, by preventing their growth and reproduction.

Anti-inflammatory agents: Onion cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch by decreasing the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals that promote inflammation.
Antioxidants: The antioxidant properties of onion cream help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate jock itch symptoms such as irritation and discoloration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Allium cepa, also known as onion, creams helps with jock itch because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredient, Allium cepa extract, helps to inhibit the growth of fungal and bacterial organisms that cause jock itch. The cream's soothing and moisturizing effects also help to calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with jock itch. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective alternative to traditional treatments, promoting healing and relief from jock itch symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: Allicin, a sulfur-containing compound, has antifungal properties that help combat the fungal infection causing jock itch, thereby providing relief from itching and discomfort.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent further fungal growth, thus aiding in the treatment of jock itch.

S-Allylcysteine sulfoxide (SACS): SACS, another sulfur-containing compound found in Allium cepa, has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control the infection and promote healing of the affected skin area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Allium cepa are used due to their antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help combat fungal infections causing jock itch.
Leaves: The leaves of Allium cepa contain compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing and treating jock itch symptoms.
Buds: The buds of Allium cepa are used for their antifungal properties, which help prevent the spread of fungal infections and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh allium cepa bulbs and thoroughly clean them with distilled water to remove dirt.
Chop the allium cepa bulbs into fine pieces and soak them in 50ml of distilled water for 30 minutes.
Heat 100ml of jojoba oil in a double boiler and add 2 tablespoons of the soaked allium cepa mixture to it.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours and then strain it through a cheesecloth to separate the oil and solids.
Combine the strained oil with 10g of beeswax and 5g of shea butter and heat the mixture in a double boiler until it reaches 180f.

remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have antifungal activity against Candida albicans, the fungus that causes jock itch, helping to reduce inflammation and promote healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in lemongrass, such as quercetin, possess anti-inflammatory effects, which help to soothe itching, redness, and swelling associated with jock itch, reducing discomfort and promoting a quicker recovery.

Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass is rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting a healthy environment for wound healing and tissue repair.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, creams helps with jock itch because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in lemongrass, such as citral and geraniol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause jock itch. The cream's cooling and soothing effects also help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with the condition. By addressing both the fungal infection and the resulting inflammation, lemongrass creams can provide effective relief from jock itch symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Citral: Citral, a terpene, has antifungal properties that help combat the fungal infections causing jock itch.
Cymbopogonol: Cymbopogonol, a sesquiterpene, exhibits antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and heal the skin affected by jock itch.
Limonene: Limonene, a terpene, has antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help prevent the growth of fungi that cause jock itch.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help combat fungal infections that cause jock itch.
Rhyzomes: Utilized for their citral content, which has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in soothing and treating jock itch.
Roots: Employed for their medicinal properties, including antifungal and antibacterial compounds that help alleviate symptoms of jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves at the peak of their fragrance and potency for best results.
Combine the dried leaves with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat for 2 hours.
Steep the mixture for 4 hours then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean container to remove solids.
Add 20g of beeswax to the oil mixture and heat it gently for 30 minutes to create a smooth consistency.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify completely before using the herbal cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with jock itch because of its potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredient, thujone, targets fungal infections such as Candida, which is a common cause of jock itch. Additionally, wormwood's antiseptic properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with the condition. As the cream penetrates deep into the skin, it provides long-lasting relief from discomfort and promotes a healthy environment for wound healing, making it an effective treatment option for individuals suffering from jock itch.
remedy_properties: Antifungal: Wormwood's antifungal properties help combat the fungal infection that causes jock itch, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch, providing relief to affected areas.
Antiparasitic: Wormwood's antiparasitic properties help eliminate the fungal parasite responsible for jock itch, reducing the risk of recurrence and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wormwood are rich in bioactive compounds that help combat fungal infections and soothe itchy skin.
Barks: The barks of wormwood contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that provide antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective against jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried artemisia absinthium leaves, aloe vera gel, coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin e oil.
Combine one part dried artemisia absinthium leaves with three parts coconut oil in a clean glass jar and let it infuse for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another glass jar and discard the solids before adding two tablespoons of beeswax and heating until melted.
Remove from heat, add one teaspoon of aloe vera gel and vitamin e oil, and stir well to combine before pouring into sterilized jars.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken completely before use, which should take approximately 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with jock itch because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredient, berberine, targets and kills the fungus that causes jock itch, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healthy skin. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe itchy and irritated skin, providing quick relief from discomfort. By addressing both the fungal infection and inflammation simultaneously, goldenseal creams offer a comprehensive solution for effectively treating jock itch.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Goldenseal contains berberine, a natural antibacterial and antifungal compound that helps combat fungal infections like jock itch.
Hydrophobic lipids: The cream's hydrophobic (water-repelling) lipids help lock in moisture and reduce fungal growth, while also providing a soothing barrier against irritation.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Goldenseal's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as berberine and isothiocyanates, may help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with jock itch.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, an antimicrobial compound that helps to reduce fungal and bacterial infections associated with jock itch.
Rhyzomes: Contain a higher concentration of alkaloids, which provide anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties to soothe itchy skin and combat infection.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 ounces of dried goldenseal root powder and 4 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Combine the goldenseal powder with 8 tablespoons of sweet almond oil in a heatproof glass bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a shallow metal pan set over simmering water for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle and discard solids.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for several hours before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with jock itch because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and saponins, work together to soothe itchy skin and reduce redness and irritation caused by fungal infections like ringworm. Additionally, dandelion's antimicrobial properties help to combat the growth of fungus and bacteria that can contribute to jock itch. By reducing inflammation and combating infection, dandelion creams provide effective relief from itching and discomfort associated with this common condition.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Dandelion's antifungal compounds help combat the fungal infection Candida albicans, which is responsible for jock itch.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion creams reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch, providing relief from discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidants help soothe and protect the affected skin, reducing damage caused by free radicals and promoting healthy tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated skin.
Rhyzomes: Containing high amounts of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit antifungal and antibacterial properties, ideal for combating fungal infections like jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean taraxacum officinale leaves for infusion taking approximately one hour.
Combine 20 grams of dried taraxacum officinale leaves with 200 milliliters of boiling distilled water steeping for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid discarding the solids and combining it with 100 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil mixing thoroughly.
Add 5 percent tea tree essential oil by volume to the infusion-oil mixture blending well taking approximately five minutes.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized glass jars sealing tightly and labeling accurately storing in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet briar creams helps with jock itch because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as salicylic acid and tea tree oil, work together to combat fungal and bacterial infections that cause itching, redness, and inflammation in the groin area. By reducing the growth of these microorganisms, sweet briar creams help alleviate symptoms and promote healing, providing effective relief from jock itch discomfort. This natural solution offers a gentle yet powerful approach to addressing this common condition.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Sweet briar cream contains antifungal compounds like salicylic acid and tannins that help combat fungal infections like jock itch, reducing inflammation and itching.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids in sweet briar cream helps to reduce swelling and redness associated with jock itch, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antimicrobial properties: Sweet briar's antimicrobial compounds, including terpenes and volatile oils, work together to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to jock itch, promoting healing and preventing further infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antifungal compounds, making them effective against fungal infections causing jock itch.
Rhyzomes: Contain antibacterial properties that help combat bacterial infections contributing to jock itch, while also soothing skin irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest rosa rubiginosa petals from healthy plants and dry them thoroughly in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days.
Grind 50 grams of dried rosa rubiginosa petals into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered rosa rubiginosa with 100 milliliters of cooled coconut oil and 20 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler, heating until fully incorporated.
Add 10 drops of tea tree essential oil for its antifungal properties and stir well to combine, allowing the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature.
Transfer the cooled cream to glass jars with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool, dry place for up to six months or refrigerate for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with jock itch because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antibacterial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, work together to reduce itching, redness, and swelling associated with fungal infections like jock itch. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's natural astringent properties help to dry out the affected area, preventing further moisture buildup that can exacerbate the condition. As a result, the cream provides fast and effective relief from the discomfort of jock itch, promoting healthy skin and a reduced risk of recurrence.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Herbal Ceylon cinnamon contains compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal and bacterial pathogens responsible for jock itch, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The active compounds in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamic acid, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching associated with jock itch.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal Ceylon cinnamon contains a high amount of antioxidants, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, promoting overall skin health and wound healing.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, also known as underground stems, contain high amounts of cinnamaldehyde, a natural antimicrobial agent that helps combat fungal infections causing jock itch.
Barks: The barks of the Ceylon cinnamon plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat skin conditions like jock itch, thanks to their antifungal and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground cinnamon with 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler set over medium heat.
Stir the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 60°c to 70°c, then remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until melted and fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers or jars, allowing it to cool and solidify completely at room temperature for about 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with jock itch because of its natural anti-fungal and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as eucalyptol and menthol, create a soothing and cooling sensation on the skin, reducing itching and inflammation. Additionally, the antimicrobial compounds in Tasmanian blue gum work to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause jock itch, promoting healthy skin and preventing recurrence of the condition. As a result, herbal Tasmanian blue gum creams provide effective relief for those suffering from jock itch.
remedy_properties: Antifungal property: Tasmanian Blue Gum cream has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity, which helps combat the fungal infection Candida albicans that causes jock itch.
Anti-inflammatory property: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms.
Antimicrobial property: Tasmanian Blue Gum's antimicrobial properties help prevent bacterial and fungal growth, promoting a healthy environment for the skin to recover from jock itch.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain compounds that exhibit antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating fungal infections like jock itch.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, which are underground stems that store nutrients, have been found to possess antibacterial and antiviral activities, helping to combat the underlying causes of jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and utensils in boiling water for at least 20 minutes.
Combine 250ml of distilled water with 25g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a saucepan set over simmering water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding solids and allowing to cool.
Mix 20g of beeswax pellets with the cooled infusion and whip until smooth and creamy taking approximately 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with jock itch because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, including linalool and linalyl acetate, work together to combat the fungal infection causing jock itch, reducing redness, itching, and irritation. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects help to alleviate discomfort and promote a sense of relaxation, making it an effective and natural remedy for treating this common skin condition.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Lavender oil's antifungal properties help combat fungal infections such as candida and athlete's foot, which can contribute to jock itch.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.

Antimicrobial properties: Lavender oil's antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria, fungi, and other microorganisms that can exacerbate jock itch, preventing further infection and speeding up recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated skin.
Flowers: Containing linalool and linalyl acetate, these essential oils help reduce fungal infections and promote a healthy skin environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the powdered lavandula angustifolia with 50g of coconut oil in a heat-proof glass bowl and stir well to mix.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water and melt the coconut oil completely stirring occasionally for 10 minutes.
Remove the melted coconut oil from the heat and let it cool to around 60°c before adding 5g of beeswax and stirring until fully incorporated taking about 3 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into a sterilized glass jar and seal tightly allowing it to solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow creams helps with jock itch because they contain salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces redness and swelling associated with the condition. The cream's antibacterial properties also help to combat fungal infections, which are common causes of jock itch. Additionally, the soothing and moisturizing effects of the cream can help to calm irritated skin, promoting healing and preventing further irritation. remedy_properties: Salicin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch.
Tannins: Tannins have antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help combat the fungal or bacterial infections that cause jock itch and prevent their spread.
Methyl Salicylate: This compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relieve itching, burning, and discomfort caused by jock itch.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contains salicylic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with jock itch.
Barks: Rich in salicin, a compound that has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective against fungal infections like jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried white willow bark salix alba and grind into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground white willow bark powder with 20ml of organic coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat stirring constantly for 30 minutes.
Add 5ml of pure beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated and melted within 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before whipping the mixture into a smooth cream using an electric mixer for 2-3 minutes.
Pour the salix alba cream into sterilized glass jars filling to the top and seal immediately while still warm.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for jock itch are topical treatments containing natural ingredients that help alleviate symptoms of jock itch, a common fungal infection causing itching, redness, and irritation in the groin area. The best herbal creams for jock itch include Melaleuca alternifolia, Aloe vera, Calendula officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Aloe barbadensis, Cymbopogon citratus, Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, and Origanum vulgare. If left untreated, jock itch can lead to discomfort, embarrassment, and disrupted daily life, causing individuals to avoid physical activity, social interactions, and even intimate relationships.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jock itch that are treated with herbal creams are ringworm, athlete's foot, and eczema. Herbal creams containing tea tree oil, neem oil, and aloe vera have been effective in treating fungal infections like these. These creams work by reducing inflammation, killing fungal spores, and promoting skin healing. Additionally, some herbal creams may also help soothe itchiness and redness associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with jock itch is a blend of Tea Tree Oil, Aloe Vera, and Coconut Oil. Tea Tree Oil has antifungal properties that combat fungal infections, while Aloe Vera soothes and calms the skin. Coconut Oil's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture, reducing itching and irritation. Additionally, incorporating Calendula and Olive Leaf extracts can enhance the healing process, as they possess natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that promote a healthy skin environment.
